lines: Find your position for sleep. [long pause] Let your body settle into the mattress. [long pause] Shift and adjust until the bed feels right beneath you — until the pillow cradles your head at an angle that feels easy, until your blanket sits where you want it. [long pause] There's no hurry. [long pause] Take whatever time you need. [long pause] The night is long enough.

Feel the weight of your body. [long pause] Your skull heavy on the pillow. [long pause] Your arms heavy at your sides. [long pause] Your legs long and heavy against the mattress. [long pause] Your whole body pressing down into the bed with the full weight of the day behind it. [long pause] Gravity is doing all the work now. [long pause] Let it.

Notice your hands. [long pause] Are they clenched? [long pause] [long pause] Even slightly? [long pause] [long pause] Most of us hold tension in our hands without knowing it — tight fists, curled fingers, rigid palms. [long pause] Open them now. [long pause] Spread your fingers gently, then let them fall into whatever position they find. [long pause] Let them be soft. [long pause] Let them hold nothing.

Notice your breathing. [long pause] Don't change it yet — just observe it. [long pause] Is it shallow? [long pause] [long pause] Is it quick? [long pause] [long pause] Sitting high in your chest? [long pause] [long pause] That's okay. [long pause] Just watch it for a few breaths, the way you'd watch a bird from a window. [long pause] No judgment. [long pause] Just noticing.

Now slow it down gently. [long pause] Breathe in through your nose for four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one, two, three, four. [long pause] Hold for three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one, two, three. [long pause] And out through your mouth for eight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight.

Again. [long pause] In for four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one, two, three, four. [long pause] Hold for three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one, two, three. [long pause] And out for eight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight. [long pause] The long exhale is the key. [long pause] It activates the parasympathetic nervous system — the part of you that says: the danger is past. [long pause] You're safe. [long pause] You can let go.

Once more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] hold... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and a long, slow out. [long pause] Good. [long pause] Let the breathing settle into whatever pace feels natural now. [long pause] You've given it a direction. [long pause] It will find its way.

Tonight we do something brave. [long pause] We practice forgiveness — of ourselves and of others. [long pause] Not because it's easy. [long pause] It may be the hardest thing you do today. [long pause] Not because the people who hurt you deserve it. [long pause] Maybe they don't. [long pause] We practice it because you deserve to sleep without carrying the weight of the day's hurts and missteps and grievances into your dreams. [long pause] Because resentment is a heavy thing to bring to bed.
Paul writes to the Ephesians: "Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry." Chapter four, verse twenty-six.

The sun has gone down. [long pause] And you may still be holding something — a frustration, a regret, a wound, a verdict you've placed on yourself or on someone else. [long pause] Tonight, we gently — gently — see if some of that can be set down. [long pause] Not forced away. [long pause] Not denied. [long pause] Just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] set down. [long pause] The way you'd set down a heavy bag at the end of a long walk.

Forgiveness is not approval. [long pause] Let that be clear from the start. [long pause] It's not saying what happened was okay. [long pause] It's not pretending it didn't hurt. [long pause] It's releasing the rope in a tug-of-war. [long pause] It's choosing not to carry this particular weight into the night.

Think back over today. [long pause] Walk through it slowly, like rewinding a film in slow motion. [long pause] Morning — waking up, getting ready, the first hours. [long pause] Midday — the middle of the day, the tasks, the conversations. [long pause] Afternoon — the fatigue, the pressure, the things that piled up. [long pause] Evening — coming home, winding down, the final hours before now.
Where does the memory snag? [long pause] [long pause] Where does the film catch, like cloth on a nail? [long pause] [long pause] There's usually something. [long pause] Maybe more than one thing.

Was there a moment you wish had gone differently? [long pause] [long pause] Something you said that came out wrong — too sharp, too dismissive, too careless? [long pause] [long pause] A reaction you'd take back if you could? [long pause] [long pause] Words that landed harder than you meant them to?

Or maybe it wasn't something you said. [long pause] Maybe it was something you didn't do. [long pause] A task you avoided. [long pause] A person you neglected. [long pause] A promise you broke — even a small one, even one you made only to yourself. [long pause] A moment where you chose the easy thing instead of the right thing.

Name it. [long pause] Simply and honestly. [long pause] Don't build a prosecution against yourself. [long pause] Don't assemble evidence. [long pause] Don't start composing the closing argument for why you're terrible. [long pause] Just name the thing. [long pause] This happened. [long pause] I wish it had gone differently.

Now hear this: you are human. [long pause] This is what it means to be human — to get it wrong sometimes. [long pause] To react from tiredness, or fear, or old patterns that don't serve you anymore. [long pause] To fall short of your own standards. [long pause] The God who made you already knows this. [long pause] He knew it before you did. [long pause] He is not surprised. [long pause] He is not tallying your failures. [long pause] He is not disappointed in the way a boss is disappointed. [long pause] He is with you in the way a parent is with a child who skinned their knee — present, compassionate, not angry.
Say this to yourself, silently, in whatever words feel true:
I did the best I could with what I had today. [long pause] I was tired. [long pause] I was human. [long pause] Where I fell short... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] I am forgiven.
I let go of the verdict I've placed on myself. [long pause] I am not the judge of my own worth tonight. [long pause] I leave that to a kinder court. [long pause] A court of mercy, not performance.

If there's a second thing — another regret, another moment that snags — name that one too. [long pause] Walk through the same release. [long pause] I did my best. [long pause] Where I fell short, I am forgiven. [long pause] I let go of the verdict.

And if there's one more — the one you almost didn't want to look at, the one that makes your chest tighten — let it come forward if it's ready. [long pause] You don't have to dig for it. [long pause] But if it's there, let it be seen.

Even this. [long pause] Especially this. [long pause] You are forgiven. [long pause] Not because you earned it. [long pause] Not because you've paid enough. [long pause] Because forgiveness is the nature of the God who made you. [long pause] It's not a transaction. [long pause] It's a quality. [long pause] Like light. [long pause] Like gravity. [long pause] It just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] is.

Is there someone who hurt you today? [long pause] [long pause] Someone whose words or actions left a mark? [long pause] [long pause] It doesn't have to be dramatic. [long pause] A careless comment from a colleague. [long pause] An unfair expectation from someone you love. [long pause] A broken promise. [long pause] Being overlooked in a room full of people. [long pause] Being dismissed when you needed to be heard. [long pause] The small wounds often sting the longest because no one else sees them.

Bring that person to mind. [long pause] Not to relive the hurt — just to acknowledge it honestly. [long pause] See their face. [long pause] Feel what you feel. [long pause] Maybe it's anger. [long pause] Maybe it's sadness. [long pause] Maybe it's that tight, hot feeling of being treated unfairly. [long pause] Whatever it is, it's real. [long pause] You don't have to pretend it isn't there.

And now a choice. [long pause] You can keep holding the rope — keep replaying the moment, keep rehearsing what you should have said, keep feeding the grievance. [long pause] Or you can let go. [long pause] Not because they deserve it. [long pause] Because holding on doesn't change what happened. [long pause] It just keeps your hands full and your body tense and your mind spinning when it should be resting.

Silently, try these words: I release you from the debt I feel you owe me. [long pause] What you did was not okay. [long pause] But I will not carry it into my sleep tonight. [long pause] I choose rest over being right.

If that felt possible — even partially, even reluctantly — that's enough. [long pause] Forgiveness doesn't require warm feelings. [long pause] It requires a decision. [long pause] The feelings may follow in time. [long pause] Or they may not. [long pause] Either way, you've loosened your grip.
Is there a second person? [long pause] [long pause] Someone else who left a mark — today or sometime recently? [long pause] [long pause] Someone whose face keeps surfacing when you're trying to rest?
Same practice. [long pause] Not approval. [long pause] Not erasure. [long pause] Just releasing the grip.

I release you. [long pause] I choose not to carry this into the night. [long pause] What happened between us is not resolved, but it doesn't need to be resolved tonight. [long pause] Tonight I choose peace.

And if either of those felt too hard — if the wound is too fresh or the hurt too deep — that's okay. [long pause] You haven't failed at forgiveness. [long pause] You can start where you are. [long pause] Like this:
I am willing to release this. [long pause] Not yet, maybe. [long pause] But I'm willing. [long pause] And the willingness is enough for tonight. [long pause] God can work with willing.
The prophet Jeremiah writes in Lamentations, chapter three: "The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases; his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning."

New every morning. [long pause] Think about that. [long pause] Not recycled mercies, stale from yesterday. [long pause] Not grudging mercies, doled out sparingly. [long pause] New ones. [long pause] Fresh ones. [long pause] As reliable as the sunrise and just as free. [long pause] Whatever happened today — the mistakes you made, the hurts you received, the things left unfinished and unresolved — none of it defines tomorrow. [long pause] The slate is wiped clean while you sleep. [long pause] The mercies are already gathering, preparing themselves for when you open your eyes.

God, I bring you the unfinished business of this day. [long pause] The things I said and wish I hadn't. [long pause] The things I should have said but didn't. [long pause] The people who hurt me and the people I hurt. [long pause] The regrets and the grievances and the tight, knotted feelings I haven't been able to untangle. [long pause] I don't have the strength to sort through all of it tonight. [long pause] So I'm leaving it here, with you. [long pause] Do with it what I can't. [long pause] Forgive where I can't forgive — not yet. [long pause] Heal what I can't heal — not alone. [long pause] And let me sleep in the mercy that will be new when morning comes. [long pause] Amen.

Now let forgiveness move through your body. [long pause] Not as an idea anymore, but as a physical release. [long pause] We'll move slowly from head to toe, and at each place, we'll let go of what the body has been holding. [long pause] Because the body keeps the day's score — every tension, every brace, every clench. [long pause] And the body deserves to be released too.

Start with your forehead. [long pause] This is where you furrow. [long pause] Where worry carves its lines. [long pause] Where you think and overthink and replay conversations and imagine future ones. [long pause] Feel the muscles there — between your brows, across the width of your forehead — and let them smooth out. [long pause] Let the forehead become wide and open and blank. [long pause] Release the day's thoughts. [long pause] They'll be there in the morning if you need them. [long pause] You don't need them now.

Your eyes. [long pause] They've been working all day — scanning screens, reading faces, watching for threats and opportunities and traffic lights and text messages. [long pause] The muscles around your eyes are some of the most active in your body. [long pause] Let them go completely now. [long pause] Your eyelids are heavy. [long pause] So heavy. [long pause] The darkness behind them is soft and welcoming and complete.

Your jaw. [long pause] This is where you hold the words you didn't say. [long pause] The retort you swallowed. [long pause] The frustration you clenched between your teeth. [long pause] The smile you forced when you didn't feel like smiling. [long pause] Let your jaw drop open. [long pause] Let your tongue fall from the roof of your mouth to the floor of your mouth. [long pause] Let the unsaid words dissolve. [long pause] You don't need to say them now. [long pause] The day is over.

Your throat. [long pause] The tightness of things held in. [long pause] Of emotions pushed down before they could surface. [long pause] Of the lump that forms when you're hurt and trying not to show it. [long pause] Let your throat open. [long pause] Let it soften. [long pause] Let whatever you've been swallowing today pass through you and out.

Your shoulders. [long pause] These carry everything. [long pause] The obligations, the expectations, other people's needs stacked on top of your own. [long pause] Feel them drop — an inch, then two. [long pause] Let them fall as far as they can go. [long pause] You are off duty now. [long pause] Whatever you were carrying, it can sit on the floor beside the bed. [long pause] It will be there in the morning. [long pause] But tonight, your shoulders are free.

Your arms. [long pause] The muscles that braced, that gripped, that held on tight. [long pause] Let them go completely slack. [long pause] Feel the heaviness pour down from your shoulders through your upper arms, your elbows, your forearms, all the way to your fingertips. [long pause] Like sand flowing slowly through an hourglass.

Your hands. [long pause] Unclench them if they've clenched again. [long pause] Open your palms to the ceiling. [long pause] These hands that grasped and reached and held and typed and gripped the steering wheel and clenched into fists under the desk — let them hold nothing. [long pause] Let them be empty and soft and still. [long pause] Open hands. [long pause] Surrendered hands.

Your chest. [long pause] Where the tight, hot feeling of anger or shame or grief lives. [long pause] Where the heart races when you're hurt or afraid. [long pause] Place your awareness there and breathe into it slowly. [long pause] Let the tightness loosen. [long pause] Let the heat cool. [long pause] Your heart rate is slowing. [long pause] Your ribcage is expanding and contracting with each easy, unhurried breath. [long pause] The storm in your chest is passing. [long pause] What's left is calm.

Your belly. [long pause] The knot of anxiety. [long pause] The pit of dread. [long pause] The weight of the day sitting right in your center like a stone. [long pause] Let it dissolve. [long pause] Not all at once — just a little softer with each exhale. [long pause] Let your belly be truly soft. [long pause] Round and relaxed and unguarded. [long pause] Rising and falling with your breath like a gentle tide coming in and going out.

Your hips and lower back. [long pause] The deep muscles that brace against the world — the ones that tighten when you feel threatened, when you feel judged, when you feel unsafe. [long pause] Let them unlock. [long pause] Let your pelvis settle heavy into the mattress. [long pause] Feel your lower back lengthen and release.

Your legs. [long pause] Thighs heavy. [long pause] Heavy as logs. [long pause] Knees soft. [long pause] Calves releasing. [long pause] All the walking, the standing, the bracing, the running — done for today. [long pause] Everything you used your legs for today is complete.

Your feet. [long pause] Still and warm. [long pause] The soles of your feet — where you stood all day on hard ground, on pavement, on floors. [long pause] Let them rest now against the soft sheets. [long pause] Warm. [long pause] Tingling slightly. [long pause] At peace. [long pause] The day's journey is over. [long pause] You've arrived.

Your whole body is now in a state of release. [long pause] Forgiven and forgiving. [long pause] Every place that was gripping has opened. [long pause] Every place that was clenching has softened. [long pause] Every place that was holding the day's hurt has let it go.

Tomorrow is clean. [long pause] Tomorrow is waiting for you with fresh mercy and fresh strength and a clean slate. [long pause] But that's tomorrow's gift. [long pause] Tonight's gift is simpler and more immediate: rest. [long pause] Deep, untroubled, forgiven rest.
The day is released. [long pause] Your body is released. [long pause] Your mind is released. [long pause] The people you forgave — released. [long pause] The verdict against yourself — released.

Let sleep come now the way dawn comes — gradually, and then all at once. [long pause] You don't have to chase it. [long pause] Just stay here, in this forgiven body, in this quiet dark, and let it find you.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
